role: Chief of Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces (2016–2025)
bio: Major General Mohammad Bagheri served as Chief of Staff of the [General Staff of the Armed Forces of Iran](/orgs/general-staff-of-the-armed-forces) from August 2016 until his death on 13 June 2025, making him the most senior uniformed officer in the Islamic Republic of Iran. An IRGC veteran of the [Iran–Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war), Bagheri rose through the IRGC intelligence and operations apparatus before being appointed to the joint command. As Chief of Staff he coordinated both the [Artesh](/orgs/artesh) and [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps), represented Iran's armed forces in international military diplomacy, and regularly appeared alongside [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) at military ceremonies. He was killed on 13 June 2025 in Israeli airstrikes on Tehran — the same strike that killed Gholam Ali Rashid — during the opening phase of what became known as the Twelve-Day War.

sources.title: Israeli airstrikes eliminate senior Iranian military officials
Iran military chief killed in Israeli strikes

editorial_notes: Born year approximate (some sources say 1956, others 1959). Killed June 13 2025 in Operation Rising Lion Israeli strikes. IRGC background but served as joint Chief of Staff.
